is how is the debt limit impacting asset
markets our answers are the US
treasury's statutory debt limit is
currently acting as a significant
Tailwind for asset markets the treasury
Department's Extraordinary Measures have
likely been exhausted and it has likely
begun financing Federal expenditures and
Debt Service with its cash balance at
the current pace of decline the TGA
balance would likely hit zero dollar
sometime in early July all told the five
things are either the following five
things are either true or likely to
become true in short order according to
our analysis us liquidity is about to
inflect into a meaningful uptrend thanks
to a likely persistent decline in the
TGA as Congress debates the merits and
contents of a single reconciliation Bill
authored by the house versus two
separate reconciliation bills authored
by the Senate to legislate president
Trump's economic agenda number two
Global liquidity is on the precipice of
a modest uptrend that may be further
accelerated by US dollar weakness if the
Trump Administration pursues a strategy
of reciprical tariffs number three the
FED is likely to end the treasury
portion of balance sheet runoff in the
first half of0 25 Number Four reported
inflation data are likely to be better
behaved in the coming months at least
until the late second quarter and number
five retail Traders are meaningfully
underweight risk assets as evidenced by
the current 16th percentile reading in
the aii Bulls bear spread we reiterate
our view that the current risk on Market
regime condition is likely to persist
throughout the first half of 2025 the
second half of 2025 is when we see the
risk from our sticky inflation and trip

Global debt refinancing air pocket we
know what those uh do to asset markets
they cause trending riskof Market
regimes typically crashes all right

and other risk management signals pick

refinancing that is what a global
refinancing a pocket is it's essentially
there's not enough Global liquidity
being created fast enough to offset the
rise in global refinancing demand and
historically that is typically
perpetuated you know trending riskof

result in in crashes and broader risk
assets S&P Bitcoin Etc and so that that
is a meaningful risk over the second
half of the year because ultimately you
know you're going to have a lot of
competing forces for that liquidity
especially if we see a significant draw

account balance they're going to have to
refill that on the other side obviously
we're not issuing net new debt debt on a

means is on the other side of that

about a United States government that
already has about 12 and a half trillion
dollars or really really somewhere close
to 1212 trillion of gross uh uh treasury
financing that needs to take place over

20th and then the second half of the
question is how quickly before the crash
